name: wildfire-exposure-eo validation AOI — Serra de Monchique (Algarve WUI)

properties.name: Serra de Monchique / Silves (2018 Algarve WUI fire)
properties.description: Validation AOI centered on the August 2018 Serra de Monchique fire (Algarve), which burned approximately 27,200 ha (secondary-sourced, see anchor_fire_source) and threatened the town of Monchique — the primary wildland-urban interface case study in southern Portugal. Copernicus EMS activation EMSR303 provides independent perimeter reference. This AOI is authored new (not promoted from an alt file); the bbox derives from docs/operationalization.md §5b ('[-8.72, 37.18, -8.38, 37.45]') expanded south and west to be clearly larger than the pilot (computed ~1771 km2 vs pilot ~902 km2, ~1.96x). Different climate zone (Mediterranean Algarve) and WUI character from the other three AOIs. Bbox adequacy VERIFIED 2026-06-16: every EMSR303 activation AOI (Marmelete in the west to Silves in the SE; map center -8.55/37.32) and the Foia massif high point (902 m, 37.3148N/8.5962W) fall inside the box with margin (anchor-based containment check, not a raw-perimeter overlay — see validation_notes).
properties.rationale: The WUI axis missing from the other three AOIs: a fire that explicitly threatened a populated urban centre (Monchique town, ~5,000 residents). Different climate zone (Mediterranean Algarve) exercises the score on a completely different fire-weather driver and fuel mix (thermophilous shrub, eucalyptus, cork-oak). The 2018 season was the second largest burned area in Algarve since records began. This AOI completes the four-AOI stress-test matrix: historical scar scale (Pedrógão), protected-area/high-mountain (Estrela + Gerês), WUI/urban-threat (Monchique).
properties.source: Bbox derived from docs/operationalization.md §5b working value, expanded to be larger than pilot; adequacy verified 2026-06-16 by anchor containment vs EMSR303 AOIs + Foia massif. Copernicus EMS EMSR303 activation page: https://mapping.emergency.copernicus.eu/activations/EMSR303/ (the older /mapping/list-of-components/EMSR303 path now redirects under /list-of-obsolete-components/). Fire event reference: ICNF Áreas Ardidas 2018 (https://www.icnf.pt/florestas/gfr/gfrint/ifiarf). Accessed 2026-06-16.
properties.anchor_fire_source: Serra de Monchique, August 2018. The ~27,200 ha figure is APPROX and secondary-sourced (VOST Portugal; Sul Informação; Safe Communities Portugal) — NOT directly confirmed from the ICNF Áreas Ardidas 2018 official record in-session (2026-06-16); treat as approximate until the ICNF canonical 2018 value is pulled directly. EMSR303 reported >3,000 ha at activation (2018-08-05). Do not average with EFFIS (different methodology, non-negotiable #(c)).
properties.copernicus_ems: EMSR303 (forest fire, Monchique/Faro; activated 2018-08-05 16:11 UTC; six AOIs Monchique/Selão/Craveiras/Marmelete/Monchique-Detail/Silves) — independent perimeter reference: https://mapping.emergency.copernicus.eu/activations/EMSR303/ . Raw delineation vector not overlaid in-session (anchor-based containment only).
properties.icnf_coverage: strong — ICNF 2018 perimeters are finalised and in the canonical annual release
properties.osm_density: moderate — Monchique town and Silves city provide infrastructure; verify before relying on asset counts
properties.validation_notes: Bbox adequacy VERIFIED 2026-06-16 (anchor containment: EMSR303 AOIs Marmelete->Silves + Foia massif all interior with margin; ~1771 km2, ~1.96x pilot). REMAINING for Phase B: (1) overlay the raw EMSR303 delineation vector / ICNF 2018 perimeter for an exact burn-intersection check; (2) confirm the ICNF-official 2018 burned-ha figure directly (current ~27,200 ha is secondary-sourced); (3) re-pick/validate the smoke tile (smoke_monchique.geojson, ~37.322N/8.540W) against actual ICNF burn intersection.
properties.wu: WU-18 (prompts/18_widen_validation.md) Phase A; bbox verified Task #3 (2026-06-16)
properties.status: verified — bbox adequacy anchor-verified vs EMSR303 AOIs + Foia massif (2026-06-16); coords unchanged. Raw-perimeter overlay + ICNF-official ha pending (Phase B).
